title: --network conflicts with --isolation=chroot, but not with BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot
body:
### Issue Description

As explained in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4257, the `--network` option doesn't do what is expected with chroot isolation, so the two CLI options were made to conflict.

However, the same does not apply to setting chroot isolation via the BUILDAH_ISOLATION environment variable. This can give a false impression that `--network=none` works, because it still has the effect of removing `/etc/resolv.conf` to break DNS resolution.

Should the chroot isolation check also apply to the environment variable? Note that this may have a larger blast radius than the CLI option, because the `quay.io/containers/buildah` image sets `BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot` by default.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `buildah build -t test --network=none --isolation=chroot` - errors as expected
2. `BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot buildah build -t test --network=none` - proceeds with the build


### Describe the results you received

`--isolation=chroot` conflicts with `--network`

`BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot` does not conflict with `--network`

### Describe the results you expected

Both ways of setting chroot isolation conflict with `--network`

### buildah version output

```yaml
Version:         1.42.2
```

### buildah info output

```yaml
Probably not relevant
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
Probably not relevant
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Yes, the CLI flag and environment variable are intended to be treated as equivalent.
I don't think I understand the concern about the container image.  We don't set a default "--network" setting in there.  Are people specifying `--network=none` when running the image, and not noticing that it doesn't have the suggested effect?

> I don't think I understand the concern about the container image. We don't set a default "--network" setting in there. Are people specifying `--network=none` when running the image, and not noticing that it doesn't have the suggested effect?

It was my case when exploring network-isolated builds. I don't have evidence to suggest that many people are doing the same, I just wanted to mention that it is common to set BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot for containerized environments. And then it will only take the `--network` flag to cause the conflict, so more people may see it than before.
